_about: Puts the HDC2450 into single-analog-line command mode on AnaCmd1 (DB25 pin 4), 0-5V center-point (0V=full reverse, 2.5V=stop, 5V=full forward) driving Motor 1. Consumed by roto_setup.py.
_warning: MOTOR POWER OFF when applying. Values land in RAM only until you pass --save. EVERY mnemonic and value below is DRAFT: verify against the HDC2450 config command reference (RoboRun+ 'Configuration' tab or the Advanced Digital Motor Controllers user manual) before --apply. Wrong config on a 2x150A controller is not a paper mistake.

_conventions.index: channel/input number as a string, or omitted for global params
_conventions.value: string; use null or "TODO" to leave a param unset (tool will SKIP it, never write a placeholder)
_conventions.note: keep the DRAFT/verify provenance here until confirmed on real hardware

items.note: DRAFT-VERIFY: command priority slot 1 = Analog. Confirm the source enum (typ. 0=none,1=Serial/USB,2=RC/Pulse,3=Analog) for this firmware.
DRAFT: slot 2 = none, so analog is the sole command authority (serial stays free for telemetry only).
DRAFT-VERIFY: analog input 1 mode. 1 = absolute (0..max maps across the range). Confirm enum.

TODO-VERIFY: analog input 1 ACTION = 'Motor Command, Channel 1'. This is a bitmask (action | channel-mask) on ADMC firmware; look up the exact encoding before setting. Left null so the tool skips it until you confirm.
DRAFT: input mV mapping to full REVERSE. Tune to your op-amp floor (a little above 0V to leave deadband headroom).
DRAFT: center (STOP) in mV = 2.5V. This is your fail-safe voltage per SafetyStage.
DRAFT: input mV mapping to full FORWARD. Keep below 5V so op-amp saturation can't peg past max.
DRAFT: analog deadband % around center — keeps the motor firmly stopped near 2.5V. Coordinate with SafetyStage deadband so they don't fight.
DRAFT: analog polarity (0=normal). Flip if forward/reverse are inverted vs your persona math.
DRAFT-VERIFY: Motor 1 operating mode. 0 = open-loop (no encoder). If you later close the loop on speed, revisit.
DRAFT: mixing mode OFF (single independent motor, not tank-mix).
DRAFT: acceleration ramp (units depend on mode). Slew for smooth ultra-slow starts — coordinate with PersonaEngine/SafetyStage slew so the gentler of the two governs.
DRAFT: motor direction (0=normal). Set on the bench once you know which way the roto should spin for 'forward'.
SET DELIBERATELY (amps): current limit for Motor 1. Fill from YOUR motor's spec — this protects motor, wiring, and battery. No safe default; left null on purpose.
SET DELIBERATELY: over-voltage limit for your bus. Left null.
SET DELIBERATELY: under-voltage limit for your bus. Left null.
